reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21 — CharsiuG2P runnability + correctness proven FIRST in headless Chrome (hello -> ˈhɛɫoʊ, bonjour -> bɔ̃ʒuʁ, спасибо -> spɐsʲibə, ありがとう -> aɾigatoɯ across 10 languages incl. non-Latin scripts), then the deployed page driven end-to-end (auto 'hello', example chips switch language + script). Text-only demo (no media).

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY + CORRECTNESS verified in headless Chrome (transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, fp32) BEFORE building. The model maps spelling -> IPA: <eng-us>:hello -> ˈhɛɫoʊ, <fra>:merci -> mɛʁsi, <ger>:danke -> ˈdaŋke, <rus>:спасибо -> spɐsʲibə, <jpn>:ありがとう -> aɾigatoɯ, <gre>:ευχαριστώ -> efxaɾisto — 10 languages incl. non-Latin scripts. Deployed page (scripts/validate-g2p.mjs 8/8): 'hello' auto-phonemized, bonjour -> bɔ̃ʒuʁ, Cyrillic спасибо phonemized. It's the model's real prediction, not a dictionary lookup.
rubric.notes: Runs off the main thread in a module Worker via T5ForConditionalGeneration + model.generate; per-word inference is well under a second on WASM.

rubric.evidence: Query '<lang-code>: word' -> IPA transcription. A DISTINCT text2text capability: it outputs PRONUNCIATION (phonemes), vs the built text2text demos (translation, summarization, grammar-correction, paraphrase, text-to-SQL, headline, byte-level fill) which all output rewritten text. G2P is the front-end step every TTS system needs.
rubric.notes: Word-level model, so the page splits a phrase into words and phonemizes each, then joins — matching how CharsiuG2P was trained.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (CharsiuG2P multilingual byT5, linked), task/pipeline (text2text-generation via transformers.js), the '<lang>: word' input format, IPA output, the byte-level tokenizer (id=byte+3, no vocab file), quantisation (fp32), download (~84 MB) and license (MIT, CharsiuG2P). No invented API.
rubric.notes: Because byT5 ships no tokenizer.json and transformers.js has no ByT5Tokenizer, the worker feeds byte ids directly via T5ForConditionalGeneration — the same documented approach as the built byt5-byte-level demo.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'Santyyy/g2p_multilingual_byT5_tiny_16_layers_100-onnx', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:84}). transformers.js caches the model files, so lib/model-cache.js sees the cached model-id path -> a returning visit auto-initialises with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~84 MB). Verified: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it flipped to ready and auto-phonemized 'hello'.
rubric.notes: Standard transformers.js runtime-owned whole-file caching, adopted via the shared loader + <model-download-status>.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (absent) -> ready (auto-phonemizes 'hello') -> debounced live phonemize on input + language change + example chips; a busy guard prevents overlapping runs; error path via the shared loader + a per-run try/catch surfaced in the status line. Verified download -> ready -> hello -> bonjour -> спасибо end-to-end.
rubric.notes: Empty input clears the output. Per-word beam decode is fast enough for live typing with a 450 ms debounce.

rubric.evidence: A text input (word or short phrase), an 18-language selector (a curated spread of CharsiuG2P's 100 codes across scripts/families), and example chips covering tricky English spellings (colonel, thorough) + non-Latin scripts (Russian, Greek, Japanese). All drive a live phonemization.
rubric.notes: The examples are chosen to teach: spelling-vs-sound mismatches and 'any script works' both show up immediately.

rubric.evidence: Every transcription is the model's real generated output; multi-word input shows the per-word breakdown so nothing is hidden. The copy is explicit that it's a TINY model decoding one word at a time, so a rare word may get a trailing artifact — the output is the model's prediction, not a dictionary lookup. No media, so no image/audio provenance concerns.
rubric.notes: Honest about the tiny-model limitation; a larger (small) variant is a recorded follow-up for higher accuracy.

rubric.evidence: The IPA output is role=status aria-live=polite; the input and language select are labelled; example chips are native buttons; the per-word breakdown is text. IPA renders in a phonetic-friendly serif stack with a system fallback.
rubric.notes: IPA glyph coverage depends on the user's fonts; the serif stack + fallback maximises the chance of correct rendering, and the raw string is always selectable/copyable.

rubric.evidence: The input row is flex-wrap (input flexes, select wraps below on narrow screens); chips + per-word breakdown wrap; the IPA output wraps on word boundaries. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate-g2p.mjs), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Text-only layout; verified desktop+mobile. Subpage ladder is a recorded follow-up.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; per-word generation (num_beams=3) is well under a second on WASM, and a 450 ms input debounce avoids redundant runs while typing. The ~84 MB fp32 download is a one-time cost.
rubric.notes: A long phrase phonemizes word-by-word sequentially (capped at 12 words); a quantized variant (if a clean one surfaces) would cut the download — recorded as a follow-up.

guidanceConsulted.query: run a transformers.js seq2seq generation model off the main thread; keep a live text input responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move inference off the main thread; keep interactions responsive (INP); debounce live input; don't block the UI on model work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — phonemization runs in a module Worker via model.generate; input is debounced 450 ms; a busy guard avoids overlapping runs.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: page responsive while typing; sub-second per word

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive input row + wrapping result that never overflows horizontally
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing; let the input flex and controls wrap; wrap long output on word boundaries.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .g2p-row is flex-wrap with a flexing input; chips + per-word list wrap; the IPA output uses word-break.

guidanceConsulted.query: announce a changing text result to assistive tech; label text input + select controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce the live result via role=status aria-live; label inputs/selects; use native controls.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the IPA output is role=status aria-live=polite; the word input + language select are labelled; examples are native buttons.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: the transcription is announced on change; controls are labelled

openQuestions: Would the 'small' CharsiuG2P variant meaningfully cut the trailing-artifact rate for the harder languages, and is the larger download worth it for a demo?
Should the page pair G2P with a TTS voice (speak the phonemes) to close the loop, or does that overreach the single-model showcase?
followUpGoals: Offer the 'small' CharsiuG2P variant as a higher-accuracy option (bigger download).
Add a 'speak it' button that feeds the phonemes to one of the built TTS demos, closing the text -> pronunciation -> audio loop.
Build a basics/practical/wild subpage ladder (spelling-vs-sound minimal pairs; homographs; rare loanwords / mixed scripts where a single still is genuinely hard).
summary: Grapheme-to-phoneme (CharsiuG2P byT5) — a distinct text2text capability (spelling -> IPA pronunciation), separate from the built translation/summarization/grammar/paraphrase/SQL/byte-fill demos, proven correct in the browser on transformers.js WASM across 10 languages and non-Latin scripts (hello -> ˈhɛɫoʊ, спасибо -> spɐsʲibə). Byte-level so any script works; MIT. Index demo complete + verified 8/8; a larger variant, a speak-it TTS link, and a subpage ladder are recorded follow-ups.
